pet.name: Lun
pet.special_needs:
Senior cat with early kidney support diet, mild arthritis, and occasional
    hairballs. Needs steady hydration monitoring, weekly weight checks,
    monthly flea prevention, and close observation for appetite drops,
    vomiting, constipation, or litter box changes.
pet.characteristics: Cats require consistent hydration access, high-quality protein-based diet with meal timing suited to individual metabolism, daily enrichment through play and environmental stimulation, regular grooming to manage shedding and hairball risk, monthly parasite prevention, and routine veterinary monitoring. Senior cats benefit from easily accessible litter boxes, soft bedding, temperature stability, and observation for changes in appetite, water intake, elimination patterns, and mobility. Weight and body condition tracking supports early detection of metabolic shifts. Cats are generally independent but thrive with predictable routines and low-stress handling.
pet.species_profile_summary: Domestic cats typically live 12-18 years in captive care; this individual requires attentive hydration, weight, and appetite monitoring alongside standard preventive and enrichment practices.

age_context.summary: Domestic cats typically live 12-18 years in captive care; this individual requires attentive hydration, weight, and appetite monitoring alongside standard preventive and enrichment practices.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Young pets benefit from short, predictable care blocks spread throughout the day. Frequent feeding, hydration checks, short play sessions, and bathroom or litter monitoring help establish routine without overwhelming the pet.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Senior pets often need lower-impact exercise, more frequent comfort checks, and closer observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility. Shorter walks, joint-friendly movement, and calm enrichment are often easier to tolerate than intense activity.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning and evening hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify water bowl is fresh and accessible; observe intake patterns for senior kidney support.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior cats with early kidney support diet require steady hydration monitoring to detect changes in water intake.

raw_recommendations.name: Appetite and food intake observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Record whether Lun ate full portions; watch for appetite drops or changes in eating speed.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Appetite changes are early indicators of metabolic shifts in senior cats and warrant veterinary attention if sustained.

raw_recommendations.name: Litter box and elimination check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe frequency, consistency, and any signs of constipation or straining; note changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior cats need close observation for changes in litter box habits, constipation, and vomiting as indicators of health shifts.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle play and calm enrichment
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer low-impact play, soft toys, or calm interaction; avoid intense activity due to mild arthritis.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from shorter, joint-friendly enrichment that improves quality of life without overexertion.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly weight and body condition check
raw_recommendations.notes: Weigh Lun and assess body condition; track trends for early detection of metabolic changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight and body condition tracking supports early detection of metabolic shifts in senior cats with kidney support needs.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle grooming and hairball management
raw_recommendations.notes: Brush gently to manage shedding and reduce hairball risk; watch for signs of discomfort.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular grooming reduces hairball risk and provides opportunity to observe mobility and comfort in senior cat with mild arthritis.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly flea prevention
raw_recommendations.notes: Apply or administer monthly flea prevention as directed; mark completion date.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Monthly flea prevention is part of routine preventive care for senior cats.

raw_recommendations.name: Watch-for-change alert: vomiting, stiffness, low energy
raw_recommendations.notes: If vomiting, constipation, appetite loss, increased stiffness, or unusual lethargy appears, contact veterinarian promptly.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior cats with kidney support diet and arthritis require close observation for changes in gait, appetite, and bathroom habits; major changes warrant veterinary consultation.
